---
title: "Procedure"
---

[← Back to Lab 2 overview](index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Working draft.** Procedure is being refined as the lab runs this quarter; expect updates day-to-day.
:::

The lab collects six step-response datasets — three parameter configurations (Baseline, Feedforward, Tuned) × two control channels (speed, yaw-rate). The same field workflow runs three times; only the parameter set changes between cases. Quality is checked at the field before each parameter-set move, so problems are caught while the boat is still in the water.

## 1. Pre-lab preparation

Get the team, software, and vehicle ready before going to the field.

- **Team roles** — pilot, ground-station operator, log curator, safety/observer.
- **Ground laptop ready** — Mission Planner installed and connected to the radio.
- **Parameter files staged** — three `.param` files corresponding to experiments A / B / C, located under `utils/ardu_params/`.
- **Vehicle ready** — battery charged, props/rudder clear, RC bound, GPS lock confirmed.
- **Test card / data sheet** — one row per dataset (six total) for noting parameter values, observations, and quality-check verdict.

## 2. Field setup

Steps to confirm at the launch site, before any data collection.

- Launch and recover the USV safely; clear of swimmers and obstructions.
- Establish telemetry link (Mission Planner connected at expected baud).
- Confirm flight mode is **ACRO**.
- Confirm logging is enabled and a new log file starts on each arm (`LOG_FILE_DSRMROT = 1`).
- Trim and zero check on RC sticks (no commanded motion at center stick).

## 3. Experiment execution

For each of the three experiments (A → B → C), repeat the same field workflow:

1. **Load the parameter set** for this experiment via Mission Planner (Config → Full Parameter Tree → Load from file).
2. **Verify** the relevant `ATC_*` parameters at the vehicle (Mission Planner shows live values).
3. **Speed step responses** — see §4.1 for stick technique.
4. **Yaw-rate step responses** — see §4.2.
5. **Field quality check** — pull the log, plot the candidate steps, decide keep / repeat (§5).
6. **Save and label** — note the log filename and parameter values on the test card.

| Experiment | Parameter changes from previous | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **A — Baseline** | Lab preset configuration; ArduRover stock PID gains; FF off; rate limiters off; throttle baseline off | First case — fully load the lab baseline `.param` |
| **B — Feedforward** | Restore `ATC_SPEED_FF` and `ATC_STR_RAT_FF` to lab values | Same PID gains as A |
| **C — Tuned** | Adjust `ATC_SPEED_P/I/D` and `ATC_STR_RAT_P/I/D` for best response | FF kept on |

## 4. Step-input technique

Manual step inputs from the RC stick, with quiet hold periods on either side of each step so the response is clean.

### 4.1 Speed (throttle) steps

- _TBD — magnitudes (e.g. 50%, 75% stick), hold time before/after, number of repeats per dataset._
- _TBD — open-water distance required for each run._

### 4.2 Yaw-rate (steering) steps

- _TBD — magnitudes (left, right, full deflection), hold time, number of repeats._
- _TBD — pre-step heading and forward speed (does the boat need to be moving?)._

### 4.3 Common gotchas

- _TBD — wind / current effect on speed steps_
- _TBD — saturation behavior (full stick → control effort clipping)_
- _TBD — minimum inter-step gap for the response to settle_

## 5. Quality check (before leaving the field)

Run the visual inspection on the laptop while the boat is still launched, so any failed dataset can be recollected on the spot.

1. Pull the latest `.BIN` log from the autopilot SD card or via Mission Planner.
2. Run [`bin2mat.py`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/utils/ardu_utils/bin2mat.py) to convert: produces a `.mat` file alongside the `.BIN`.
3. Run [`proc_closedloop_response.m`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/book/w07_lab_usv_pid/code/proc_closedloop_response.m) in MATLAB to plot the timeseries.
4. Check each step in the figure:
   - **Setpoint reaches the commanded value** (no rate-limited ramp instead of a step)
   - **Measured response settles** to a steady value within ~10 s
   - **Control effort is not pinned** at ±1 for an extended period (saturation)
   - **PIDT / PIDS log channels are present** (PID-internal logging is on)
   - **No long gaps** in the GPS or STER sample stream

If any check fails for the dataset just collected → recollect before moving on. If it's a parameter or vehicle issue → resolve, then continue.

## 6. End-of-day

Wrap up the field session.

- All six `.mat` files transferred to the team's shared location.
- Test card filled in (one row per dataset, with quality-check verdict).
- Vehicle returned to nominal parameter configuration (rate limiters restored, FF restored, throttle baseline restored) — load a `.param` file with the post-lab nominal values.
- Battery on charger, vehicle stowed.

## 7. Hand-off to next week

Datasets and notes are the input to the Week-8 analysis: process each `.mat` with [`closedloop_stepinfo.m`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/book/w07_lab_usv_pid/code/closedloop_stepinfo.m) to extract rise time, settling time, overshoot, and steady-state error, then compare across the three experiments.
